title: B2B E-commerce Platforms 2026: Top Solutions for Wholesale and Business-to-Business Selling
description: Compare B2B e-commerce platforms for wholesale and business-to-business selling. Evaluate features like custom pricing, quote management, company accounts, a...
content:
B2B E-commerce: Distinct Requirements

B2B e-commerce differs fundamentally from consumer (B2C) selling, requiring platforms with features addressing business buyer needs: bulk ordering, custom pricing tiers, quote negotiations, company accounts with multiple users, purchase order processing, and ordering workflow approvals. In 2026, B2B commerce platforms mature significantly, providing sophisticated functionality enabling manufacturers, wholesalers, and distributors to serve business customers through digital self-service.

Business buyers expect consumer-like convenience with B2B-specific capabilities. Modern B2B platforms blend intuitive interfaces familiar from B2C shopping with business features like custom catalogs, negotiated pricing, invoice payment, and integration with procurement systems. The right B2B platform balances usability with business functionality.

Top 10 B2B E-commerce Platforms

These platforms provide strong B2B commerce capabilities:

PlatformB2B FeaturesCustom PricingQuote SystemCompany AccountsPricing Tier
Magento (Adobe Commerce)ExcellentAdvancedNativeFullEnterprise
BigCommerce EnterpriseExcellentAdvancedNativeFullCustom
Shopify PlusGrowingScriptsAppLimited$2,000/mo
Salesforce CommerceGoodYesNativeYesEnterprise
Optimizely (formerly Episerver)ExcellentYesYesYesEnterprise
InsiteCommerceExcellentAdvancedNativeFullEnterprise
4CommerceExcellentYesYesYesCustom
Woocommerce B2BPluginPluginPluginPluginFree
PrestaShop B2BModuleModuleModuleModuleFree
OrderoroPurpose-builtYesYesYes$500/mo

Essential B2B Platform Features

B2B commerce requires specific functionality rarely needed in B2C selling. Understanding these requirements guides platform selection.

Custom Pricing and Price Lists

Business pricing varies by customer relationship, order volume, negotiated contracts, and customer groups. Platforms must support:

  • Customer-specific pricing: Individual negotiated rates per product
  • Group pricing tiers: Discount levels by customer category
  • Volume pricing: Quantity-based price breaks
  • Contract pricing: Pre-negotiated rates for contract periods
  • List price visibility: Show MSRP alongside customer's discounted price
  • Hide pricing: Require login to view prices (common in B2B)
  • Multi-tier markup: Different margins per product category

Magento and BigCommerce Enterprise provide sophisticated native price list management. Shopify Plus handles custom pricing through checkout scripts, requiring more manual configuration.

Quote Management Systems

B2B purchases often require quote negotiation before ordering. Quote systems enable:

  • Quote request submission: Customer requests pricing on products
  • Quote creation and modification: Merchant prepares quote document
  • Quote negotiation workflow: Back-and-forth pricing discussions
  • Quote to order conversion: Accepted quotes become orders
  • Quote expiration management: Time limits on quote validity
  • Quote history tracking: Previous quotes for reference

Magento includes comprehensive native quote management. BigCommerce Enterprise provides quote functionality. Shopify Plus requires apps like Request a Quote for B2B quotes.

Company Accounts and User Management

Business customers have multiple employees ordering under one company account. Platform features include:

  • Company account creation with business information
  • Multiple user accounts under company umbrella
  • Role-based permissions (buyer, approver, admin)
  • Order approval workflows before submission
  • Shared order history across company users
  • Spending limits by user or role
  • Address book management for multiple delivery locations
FeatureMagentoBigCommerceShopify Plus
Company AccountsFull nativeFull nativeLimited/App
Multiple UsersUnlimitedUnlimitedLimited
Role PermissionsAdvancedAdvancedBasic
Approval WorkflowNativeNativeFlow/App
Spending LimitsYesYesApp
Shared AddressesYesYesLimited

Magento provides most sophisticated company account management. BigCommerce Enterprise offers strong capabilities. Shopify Plus B2B functionality is improving but less mature.

Bulk Ordering Capabilities

Business buyers order multiple products simultaneously, requiring efficient bulk ordering tools:

  • Quick order forms: SKU-based entry without browsing
  • CSV/Excel import: Upload product lists for ordering
  • Order from previous orders: Reorder from history
  • Product list favorites: Saved product selections
  • Multi-quantity entry: Enter quantities across many products
  • Bulk add to cart: Add entire categories or saved lists
  • Purchase order upload: Import PO files

All major B2B platforms support bulk ordering, though interface efficiency varies. Magento's quick order form provides SKU entry with autocomplete. BigCommerce offers bulk order functionality.

Payment Methods for B2B

B2B payment differs from consumer transactions, requiring platform support:

  • Invoice payment: Pay on terms (NET 30, NET 60)
  • Purchase order processing: PO numbers for accounting
  • Credit line management: Pre-approved customer credit
  • ACH/bank transfer: Direct payment integration
  • Credit card: Standard card processing
  • Partial payment: Deposit plus balance payment
  • Payment on account: Customer account balance system

Invoice payment with terms requires customer credit management. Magento and BigCommerce Enterprise support invoice payment and terms. Shopify Plus requires apps for invoice functionality.

Magento B2B Commerce

Magento (Adobe Commerce) provides the most comprehensive native B2B functionality. The platform handles sophisticated wholesale and manufacturing requirements without requiring extensive extensions.

Magento B2B Capabilities

  • Company accounts with full hierarchy management
  • Native quote request and negotiation system
  • Advanced price list management per customer/group
  • Quick order forms and bulk ordering tools
  • Order approval workflows configurable
  • Purchase order processing
  • Invoice payment with NET terms
  • Shared catalogs for different customer groups
  • Requisition lists (saved product lists)
  • Negotiable quote attachments and comments

Magento B2B functionality works for both B2C and B2B simultaneously through shared catalog system. Different customer groups see appropriate catalogs and pricing.

Magento B2B Implementation

Magento B2B requires Adobe Commerce license (Magento Open Source lacks official B2B features, though plugins approximate functionality). Implementation demands:

  • Adobe Commerce license ($50,000-200,000/year)
  • B2B module activation (included in Commerce)
  • Custom development for specific workflows ($20,000-100,000)
  • Integration with ERP for inventory and pricing ($10,000-50,000)
  • Performance optimization for catalog scale ($5,000-20,000)

Magento B2B suits manufacturers, wholesalers, and distributors with complex requirements and substantial budgets.

BigCommerce Enterprise B2B

BigCommerce Enterprise provides robust B2B functionality at lower cost than Magento. The platform's native features reduce extension dependency while handling most B2B needs.

BigCommerce B2B Features

  • Company accounts with multiple users
  • Price lists for customer groups and individuals
  • Quote system native in platform
  • Bulk ordering through quick order forms
  • Order approval workflows
  • Custom pricing display rules
  • Invoice payment capability
  • B2B wholesale channel separate from B2C storefront
  • Purchase order processing
  • Shared address books

BigCommerce enables separate wholesale storefront while sharing inventory and backend with consumer store. This separation maintains different UX for B2B and B2C customers.

BigCommerce B2B Value

BigCommerce Enterprise provides B2B functionality at significantly lower cost than Magento:

  • Custom pricing (negotiate based on requirements)
  • No transaction fees reducing payment costs
  • Native B2B features without B2B-specific license
  • Lower implementation costs than Magento
  • Managed platform reducing technical burden

Annual BigCommerce Enterprise costs typically range $20,000-80,000, substantially below Magento's $50,000-200,000+ range.

Shopify Plus B2B Capabilities

Shopify Plus B2B functionality has improved significantly, now handling basic B2B needs through native features and apps. The platform suits businesses transitioning from B2C to B2B or with moderate B2B complexity.

Shopify Plus B2B Components

  • Wholesale channel: Separate catalog and pricing
  • Checkout scripts: Custom pricing logic
  • Flow: Workflow automation including approvals
  • Company accounts: Available with limitations
  • B2B apps: Extensions for quotes, PO, invoice

Shopify Plus wholesale channel provides B2B-specific storefront with custom pricing, minimum order quantities, and simplified checkout. However, complex B2B workflows require app extensions.

Shopify Plus B2B Limitations

  • Quote system requires apps
  • Company account functionality limited compared to Magento
  • Invoice payment on terms requires apps
  • Custom pricing configuration more manual
  • Complex approval workflows need Flow customization
  • Multi-catalog support limited

Shopify Plus works well for straightforward B2B selling with simple pricing and ordering flows. Complex manufacturing or wholesale operations may exceed Shopify's capabilities.

Specialized B2B Platforms

Purpose-built B2B platforms target specific industry needs.

InsiteCommerce

InsiteCommerce provides B2B commerce specifically for manufacturers and distributors, with deep ERP integration capabilities.

  • Manufacturing and distribution focus
  • Deep ERP integration (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics)
  • Configurator integration for custom products
  • Complex product catalog handling
  • Field sales rep tools
  • Multi-location inventory visibility

Optimizely Commerce

Optimizely (formerly Episerver) combines content management with B2B commerce, ideal for content-rich B2B websites.

  • Strong content-commerce integration
  • Personalization capabilities
  • B2B-specific features
  • Multi-language, multi-market
  • Enterprise integration

Integration Requirements

B2B commerce requires deep integration with business systems, different from B2C's marketing-focused integrations.

ERP Integration

ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics, NetSuite) provide inventory, pricing, customer credit, and product master data:

  • Real-time inventory synchronization
  • Customer credit status for invoice approval
  • Product pricing from ERP contracts
  • Order submission to ERP for fulfillment
  • Customer master data synchronization
PlatformERP IntegrationImplementation
MagentoStrong APICustom ($20-50K)
BigCommerceAPI + partnersMiddleware ($10-30K)
Shopify PlusApps/APIApps + custom ($5-20K)
InsiteCommerceNativeBuilt-in

Other B2B Integrations

  • CRM: Customer relationship data (Salesforce, HubSpot)
  • PIM: Product information management for complex catalogs
  • WMS: Warehouse management for multi-location fulfillment
  • EDI: Electronic data interchange for wholesale trading
  • Procurement systems: Punch-out catalog integration
  • Accounting: Invoice and payment reconciliation

Punch-Out Catalog Integration

B2B buyers using procurement systems (Coupa, SAP Ariba) require punch-out catalog integration. This capability enables buyers to shop your catalog directly within their procurement platform:

  • cXML punch-out protocol support
  • OCI (Open Catalog Interface) integration
  • Round-trip order data exchange
  • Procurement system authentication

Magento and specialized B2B platforms support punch-out. BigCommerce and Shopify Plus require middleware or apps for punch-out integration.

B2B UX Design Considerations

B2B interface design differs from B2C aesthetics, prioritizing efficiency over visual appeal.

B2B UX Priorities

  • Quick ordering efficiency over browsing experience
  • Product information depth over lifestyle imagery
  • Account management over social features
  • Order history accessibility
  • Technical specifications prominence
  • Bulk order interfaces
  • Mobile support for field ordering

B2B buyers know what they want; they need efficient ordering, not discovery. Platforms should enable quick order entry, easy access to saved product lists, and streamlined checkout without unnecessary steps.

Choosing B2B Platform

Select B2B platform based on business complexity, integration needs, and budget:

  • Complex manufacturing/distribution: Magento for comprehensive B2B functionality
  • Growing wholesaler with moderate complexity: BigCommerce Enterprise for value
  • Simple B2B selling, Shopify familiarity: Shopify Plus wholesale channel
  • Deep ERP integration required: InsiteCommerce or similar specialized platform
  • Content-rich B2B: Optimizely for content-commerce blend
  • Budget constraint: WooCommerce with B2B plugins (limited scalability)

B2B platform selection impacts operational efficiency significantly. Test platforms through B2B-specific demos, evaluate integration capabilities, and verify company account features before committing. The right B2B platform streamlines wholesale operations; wrong choice creates friction frustrating business customers.
author: E-commerce Specialist

faq.question: What features make a platform suitable for B2B commerce?
faq.answer: B2B commerce platforms require features like custom pricing (customer-specific rates, volume pricing, negotiated contracts), quote management (request, negotiate, convert to order), company accounts with multiple users and role permissions, order approval workflows, bulk ordering tools (quick order forms, CSV import), invoice payment with NET terms, purchase order processing, and ERP integration for inventory and pricing synchronization. These features address business buyer needs different from consumer purchasing.

faq.question: Can Shopify handle B2B selling?
faq.answer: Shopify Plus handles basic B2B selling through its wholesale channel providing separate B2B storefront with custom pricing and minimum order requirements, checkout scripts for pricing logic, and Flow for approval workflows. However, complex B2B features like advanced quote negotiation, sophisticated company account hierarchies, invoice payment on terms, and punch-out catalog integration require apps or custom development. Shopify Plus suits straightforward B2B with simple pricing, not complex wholesale operations.

faq.question: How does B2B pricing differ from B2C pricing on e-commerce platforms?
faq.answer: B2B pricing differs through custom pricing tiers per customer or customer group (wholesale, distributor, retail), negotiated contract pricing stored in the platform, volume-based price breaks (higher quantity = lower price), price hiding requiring login before viewing, showing list price alongside customer's discounted price, and multi-tier markup structures. B2C uses single public pricing for all customers. B2B platforms manage complex price lists for different customer relationships, often synchronized from ERP contract data.
